properties.title: Abae
properties.description: Abai was an ancient town in Phocis, said to have been founded by the Argive Abas.

properties.title: Abdera
properties.description: Abdera was a coastal city located on Cape Bulustra, near the estuary of the river Nestos.

properties.title: Abodiacum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 C2 Abodiacum

properties.title: Abrincas
properties.description: Ingena/Abrincas (modern Avranches) was the chief settlement of the Abrincatui tribe.

properties.title: Abydus
properties.description: A city of Mysia located on the Hellespont.

properties.title: Acelum
properties.description: Acelum (modern Asolo) was an ancient settlement of the Veneti.

properties.title: Aceruntia
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 45 C3 Ac(h)eruntia

properties.title: Acholla
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 33 H2 Acholla

properties.title: Acinippo
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 26 E5 Acinippo

properties.title: Acrae
properties.description: Akrai, an ancient city of Sicily, originated as a colony founded by Syracuse in 664 B.C., making it the first of the city's three colonial foundations.

properties.title: Aecae
properties.description: An ancient town of Apulia that in legend was reported to have been founded by Diomedes. In the third century B.C. the Roman general Quintus Fabius Maximus captured the town after the battle of Cannae.

properties.title: Aeso
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 G3 Aeso

properties.title: Agedincum
properties.description: A settlement located at the Yonne-Vanne confluence in the territory of the Senones.

properties.title: Aggar
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 33 G1 Aggar?

properties.title: Agyrium
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 47 F3 Agyrium

properties.title: Alauna
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 7 E2 Alauna

properties.title: Alba Helviorum
properties.description: Alba Helviorum was the capital of the Helvii in Gallia Narbonensis.

properties.title: Alba Pompeia
properties.description: Alba Pompeia (modern Alba) was a Celtic and Ligurian settlement that entered into the Roman sphere during the late Republican period. In Imperial times Alba was the birthplace of Pertinax.

properties.title: Albona
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 20 B4 Albona

properties.title: Alebaece Reiorum Apollinarium
properties.description: Alebaece Reiorum Apollinarium/Apollinaris Reiorum/Reii was the capital of the tribe of the Reii. Modern Riez in France's Alpes-de-Haute-Provence department.

properties.title: Alesia
properties.description: A Gallic oppidum, site of Caesar's defeat of Vercingetorix, and afterwards a Gallo-Roman town

properties.title: Alveria
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 20 C5 Alveria

properties.title: Amantia
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 49 B3 Amantia

properties.title: Amasia
properties.description: Amaseia (modern Amasya) was a naturally fortified settlement in the Pontic mountains near the Black Sea. It served as the capital of the kingdom of Pontus from ca. 300 BC to 183 BC.

properties.title: Amisus
properties.description: A colony of Miletus that eventually came to be part of the kingdom of Pontus.

properties.title: Amyzon
properties.description: An ancient settlement in Caria and one of the cities in the Chrysaorian League of Carian cities. By Strabo's time it was deemed a suburb of Alabanda.

properties.title: Anaphe
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 B5 Anaphe

properties.title: Anderitum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 A4 Anderitum

properties.title: Andros
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 60 A4 Andros

properties.title: Angitiae Lucus
properties.description: Angitiae Lucus refers to the grave of Angitia, a divine figure of the Marsi tribe.

properties.title: Ansium
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 20 C5 Ansium

properties.title: Antigonia
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 49 C3 Antigoneia

properties.title: Antiochia ad Cragum
properties.description: Antiochia ad Cragum is a city founded in 170 B.C.

properties.title: Antiphellus
properties.description: Antiphellos/Habesos was an ancient settlement in Lycia.

properties.title: Antipolis
properties.description: Antipolis (modern Antibes) was founded as a colony from Massilia in the fifth century BC and it emerged as a key trading center. In the late first century BC the city was incorporated into the Roman province of Gallia Narbonensis.

properties.title: Anxa
properties.description: Kallipolis/Anxa in legend was said to have been founded by Idomeneus of Crete, while Pliny the Elder credits Senonian Gauls as founders. It is most likely that the site was a Messapic settlement.

properties.title: Aphrodisias
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 51 H3 Aphrodisias/Col. Flaviopolis

properties.title: Apollonia
properties.description: An ancient settlement in the modern S. Fratello commune in northern Sicily.

properties.title: Apollonia
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 65 C5 Apollonia

properties.title: Aprustum
properties.description: Aprustum was an interior town of Bruttium.

properties.title: Apsarus
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 20 B5 Apsarus

properties.title: Apta Iulia
properties.description: Once the most important town of the Gallic Vulgientes, Romans destroyed the site ca. 125 BC and Iulius Caesar re-founded it as Apta Iulia.

properties.title: Aptera
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 60 B2 Aptera

properties.title: Aquae Mattiacorum
properties.description: Aquae Mattiacorum (modern Wiesbaden) was the capital of the Civitas Mattiacorum.

properties.title: Aquae Statiellae
properties.description: Aquae Statiellae (modern Acqui Terme) was an ancient settlement in Liguria.

properties.title: Aquae Tarbellicae
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 D2 Aquae Terebellicae

properties.title: Aquilonia
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 45 B2 Aquilonia

properties.title: Aradus
properties.description: Arados (modern Arwad, Syria) is located on Ruad Island in the Syrian Sea. The island was first settled by Phoenicians in the second millennium BC.

properties.title: Arae Philaenorum
properties.description: A site located near the bottom of the Great Syrtis which marked the boundary between the territories of Carthage and Cyrene, and later between Tripolitana and Cyrenaica.

properties.title: Arba
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 20 B5 Arba

properties.title: Aregenua
properties.description: Aregenoua was the chief city of the civitas of the Viducasses.

properties.title: Argyruntum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 20 C5 Argyruntum

properties.title: Arsinoe
properties.description: An ancient city in the Heptanomis and the capital of Arsinoites nome. The ancient Egyptian name of the site was Shedyet and in Pharaonic times it was a major cult center for the god Sobek.

properties.title: Arupium
properties.description: Arupium (modern Prozor, Croatia) was a town of the Iapydes in Illyricum.

properties.title: Asseria
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 20 C5 Asseria

properties.title: Astypalaea
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 C4 Astypalaia

properties.title: Ateste
properties.description: Ateste was a city of northern Italy, located in the interior of Venetia.

properties.title: Atria
properties.description: (H)Atria (modern Adria) was a Venetic and, later, Etruscan settlement. The city itself is likely a sixth century BC foundation.

properties.title: Atuatuca Tungrorum
properties.description: Atuatuca (modern Tongeren, Belgium) was also known as Aduatuca Tungrorum or Atuatuca Tongrorum and served as the capital of the Civitas Tungrorum.

properties.title: Augusta Bagiennorum
properties.description: Augusta Bagiennorum was a chief settlement of the Bagienni, a Ligurian tribe. A Roman colony was established at the site in the time of Augustus. Remains of structures are found to the northeast of the modern town of Bene Vagienna, Italy.

properties.title: Augusta Cilicia
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 66 G2 Augusta/Augustopolis/[Thebae]?

properties.title: Augusta Suessionum
properties.description: Augusta Suessionum (modern Soissons) was a Gallic site captured by Iulius Caesar and re-established as a Roman city.

properties.title: Augusta Viromanduorum
properties.description: Augusta Viromanduorum was a Roman settlement that replaced a pre-Roman oppidum of the Viromandui.

properties.title: Augustobona
properties.description: Augustobona Tricassium stood at a key highway hub on the Via Agrippa.

properties.title: Augustobriga
properties.description: Augustobriga was an ancient city located on the left bank of the river Tagus, between Emerita Augusta and Toletum.

properties.title: Augustodunum
properties.description: Augustodunum was an Augustan foundation located at the point where the river basins of the Loire, Saône, and Rhône rivers meet. Modern Autun.

properties.title: Augustodurum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 7 F2 Augustodurum

properties.title: Augustomagus
properties.description: Augustomagus (modern Senlis) was located along the road between between Caesaromagus and Suessona.

properties.title: Aulon
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 49 B3 Aulon

properties.title: Ausculum
properties.description: Ausculum (Ascoli Satriano) was a settlement of the Daunians.

properties.title: Autricum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 B4 Autricum

properties.title: Avennio
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 15 D2 Avennio

properties.title: Baesuris
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 26 C4 (B)Aesuris/Esuris

properties.title: Bagacum
properties.description: Bagacum was an ancient city located in Gallia Belgica and a capital of the Nervii.

properties.title: Baiae
properties.description: A Roman seaside community on the Bay of Naples that included thermal springs.

properties.title: Bantia
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 45 D3 Bantia

properties.title: Barium
properties.description: A city of the Peucetii that came under Roman control in the third century BC.

properties.title: Beda
properties.description: An ancient settlement of the Treveri. Modern Bitburg in Germany.

properties.title: Bellunum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 E3 Bellunum

properties.title: Beneharnum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 E2 Bene(h)arnum

properties.title: Bocchori
properties.description: A pre-Roman and Roman settlement on the island of Majorca. Its ruins are known as Pedret de Bóquer, which is located at the modern Port de Pollença. The original settlement dates ca. 1400 BC.

properties.title: Boutae
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 G2 Boutae

properties.title: Brigantium
properties.description: Brigantium (modern Bregenz) was a Celtic settlement from at least the fifth century B.C. and came under Roman control in the first century B.C.

properties.title: Brixellum
properties.description: An ancient Roman town in the plain of the Po river.

properties.title: Bubastis
properties.description: Boubastis (also known, in Arabic, as Tell Basta or, in Egyptian, as Per-Bast) was an ancient city in Egypt. It was located in the Nile Delta and served as the capital of the Am-Khent nome (the Bubastite nome), which was the 18th nome of Lower Egypt.

properties.title: Bulla Regia
properties.description: A center for Numidian royalty, Bulla Regia first became an oppidum liberum in the reign of Augustus and then a colony under Hadrian. The site, which boasts significant archaeological remains, lies in modern Tunisia.

properties.title: Busiris
properties.description: Bousiris was a city of Lower Egypt near the Phatnitic mouth of the Nile river and was considered one of the possible birthplaces of Osiris.

properties.title: Buthrotum
properties.description: Modern Butrint (Butrinti) in Albania, ancient Bouthroton was an ancient Greek and later Roman city located in Epirus. The city became an important bishopric by the sixth century.

properties.title: Butua
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 49 A1 Bouthoe

properties.title: Butuntum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 45 E2 Butuntum

properties.title: Buxentum
properties.description: An ancient coastal settlement of south Italy, modern Policastro Bussentino.

properties.title: Cabellio
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 15 E2 Cabellio

properties.title: Cabilonnum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 18 B3 Cabilonnum

properties.title: Cadyanda
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 65 B4 Kadyanda

properties.title: Caelia
properties.description: A town of the Peucetii in Apulia.

properties.title: Caesarobriga
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 27 A2 Caesarobriga

properties.title: Caesaromagus
properties.description: Caesaromagus was the civitas capital of the Bellovaci.

properties.title: Caesaromagus
properties.description: An 8 hectare Romano-British settlement that developed beginning in the 60s AD and continued in use at least until the fourth century.

properties.title: Caesena
properties.description: An Umbrian and Etruscan city on the Savio river that passed into Roman control in the third century B.C.

properties.title: Camaracum
properties.description: Camaracum was the capital of the civitas of the Nervii.

properties.title: Candyba
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 65 C5 Kandyba

properties.title: Cannae
properties.description: An ancient settlement of Apulia on the Aufidus river. Nearby the Roman army suffered a major defeat at the hands of the Carthaginians in 216 B.C. during the Second Punic War.

properties.title: Capitolias
properties.description: A city of the Decapolis in northern Jordan.

properties.title: Carcaso
properties.description: A fortified Roman and Medieval site with occupation beginning from the fourth millennium B.C.

properties.title: Carreum Potentia
properties.description: Carreum Potentia (Regio IX) is the site of a Roman colony, perhaps planted during the Gracchan period, on a site that preserves, perhaps, its pre-Roman name.

properties.title: Carthaea
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 58 G2 Karthaia

properties.title: Cassandrea
properties.description: Poteidaia/Kassandreia was a Greek colony founded ca. 600 BC by Corinthians on the peninsula of Pallene in western Chalcidice. The site received a Roman colony in 43 B.C.

properties.title: Cassope
properties.description: A city with an extensive circuit wall, temple, theater, and agora.

properties.title: Castabus
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 G4 Kastabos

properties.title: Castrimoenium
properties.description: A Latin settlement that became a Roman municipium.

properties.title: Cedreae
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 G4 Kedreai

properties.title: Centum Cellae
properties.description: Centum Cellae (modern Civitavecchia) was an ancient coastal settlement in Tyrrhenian Etruria. The emperor Trajan re-builds the harbor at the site in 106/7 and Pliny the Younger mentions its name, Centum Cellae.

properties.title: Ceramus
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 F3 Keramos

properties.title: Cetium
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 12 I4 Cetium

properties.title: Cirrha
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 C4 Kirra

properties.title: Cissa
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 20 B5 Cissa

properties.title: Claterna
properties.description: Claterna was a Roman a town of Gallia Cispadana, located along the Via Aemilia between Bononia and Forum Cornelii.

properties.title: Clipea
properties.description: Aspis/Clipea, located on the Cap Bon peninsula, was founded in the fifth century BC by the Carthaginians. Site of the Siege of Aspis (255 BC), the first land battle in Africa during the First Punic War.

properties.title: Cluana
properties.description: Cluana was an ancient settlement in Picenum.

properties.title: Colybrassus
properties.description: An ancient city of Cilicia Trachea.

properties.title: Compsa
properties.description: Compsa was a city of the Hirpini, located near the sources of the Aufidus river.

properties.title: Condate Redonum
properties.description: The primary center of the Redones tribe. Modern Rennes in France.

properties.title: Confluentes
properties.description: Confluentes (Koblenz) was a Roman military outpost established by Drusus ca. 8 BC.

properties.title: Consentia
properties.description: The capital city of the ancient Bruttii in Calabria.

properties.title: Consoranni
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 G2 Consoranni

properties.title: Corinium
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 20 C5 Corinium

properties.title: Cornus
properties.description: Cornus was an ancient settlement on the west coast of Sardinia. The site is located in the modern municipal territory of S. Caterina di Pittinuri.

properties.title: Corseae
properties.description: The modern town of Fournoi on the largest of the Aegean islands of the same name was likely the principal settlement and harbor of the archipelago, inheriting from them the name Korsiai.

properties.title: Corydalla
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 65 D5 Korydalla

properties.title: Cos
properties.description: An ancient city, located on the Aegean island of the same name.

properties.title: Cosedia
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 7 E2 Cosedia/Constantia

properties.title: Cosilinum
properties.description: Co(n)silinum/Cosilianum was a statio on the Via Popilia (Annia) in ancient Calabria.

properties.title: Cossyra
properties.description: A harbor settlement on the island of the same name.

properties.title: Crexa
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 20 B5 Crexa

properties.title: Cularo
properties.description: Cularo/Gratianopolis was a city of Gallia Narbonensis in the territory of the Allobroges on the Isère river.

properties.title: Curia
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 B3 Curia

properties.title: Curicum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 20 B4 Curicum

properties.title: Curubis
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 32 G3 Curubis

properties.title: Cussae
properties.description: An ancient city of Egypt on the west bank of the Nile, known as Qis or Kis in Egyptian, Cusae or Kusai in Greek. The modern associated location id El Qusiya.

properties.title: Cynopolis
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 75 D4 Kynopolis/Sko/Euergetis

properties.title: Dardanium
properties.description: Dardanos was a city of the Troad.

properties.title: Darioritum
properties.description: The primary city of the Veneti in Gallia Lugdunensis (modern Vannes).

properties.title: Decem Pagi
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 G4 Decem Pagi

properties.title: Decetia
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 I2 Decetia

properties.title: Delos
properties.description: The ancient settlement of Delos, located on the Aegean island of the same name.

properties.title: Dibio
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 18 C2 Castrum Divionense

properties.title: Diospolis Inferior
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 74 F2 Diospolis Kato

properties.title: Divodurum
properties.description: Divodurum/Mettis was a Celtic capital oppidum of the Mediomatrici tribe and was captured by Iulius Caesar in 52 BC.

properties.title: Dodona
properties.description: An ancient site of northwestern Epirus, Dodona was home to one of the most ancient oracular traditions of the Hellenic world.

properties.title: Drepanum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 A3 Drepanon?

properties.title: Drepanum
properties.description: Drepanum, the modern Trapani, Sicily, is an ancient port city. The archaeological remains of the ancient city are meager. During the First Punic War, Hamilcar fortified the site.

properties.title: Durobrivae
properties.description: Durobrivae was a fortified Roman garrison town that was located at what is now Water Newton, Cambridgeshire. The fort was placed at the point where Ermine Street crossed the River Nene.

properties.title: Durobrivae
properties.description: Roman town at present day Rochester, cited: BAtlas 8 H3 Durobrivae

properties.title: Egnatia
properties.description: Egnatia/Gnathia was an ancient city of the Messapii in southern Italy.

properties.title: Elimberrum Augusta Auscorum
properties.description: Elimberrum was the capital of the civitas of the Auscii.

properties.title: Epamanduodurum
properties.description: Epamanduodurum was a Roman settlement along the road to Vesontio.

properties.title: Epidaurus
properties.description: Epidauros is located on a small peninsula on the eastern coast of Peloponnesus.

properties.title: Equus Tuticus
properties.description: Aequum Tuticum/Equus Tuticus was an ancient Oscan town. The settlement was located near the intersection of the Via Traiana and the Via Herculia in the territory of the Hirpini.

properties.title: Ercavica
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 C5 Ercavica

properties.title: Erythrae
properties.description: Erythrai was one of the twelve Ionian cities of Asia Minor. It is located on a small peninsula that extends into the Bay of Erythrae.

properties.title: Euhippe
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 G2 Euhippe

properties.title: Euromus
properties.description: Europos/Euromos/Philippoi was a settlement of interior Caria.

properties.title: Fanum Martis
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 D2 Fanum Martis

properties.title: Faventia
properties.description: A Roman city on the Via Aemilia.

properties.title: Feltria
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 D3 Feltria

properties.title: Fidenae
properties.description: Fidenae was an ancient city of Latium.

properties.title: Flaviobriga
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 B2 Flaviobriga

properties.title: Flaviopolis
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 86 C3 Krateia/Flaviopolis/*Agrippeia?

properties.title: Flavium Brigantium
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 24 C1 (Flavium) Brigantium/Portus Magnus?

properties.title: Forentum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 45 C2 Forentum?

properties.title: Forum Cassii
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 42 C4 Forum Cassii

properties.title: Forum Cornelii
properties.description: An ancient city founded by Lucius Cornelius Sulla as a colony ca. 82 BC.

properties.title: Forum Fulvii Valentinum
properties.description: Forum Fulvii Valentinum was a small Roman settlement in Liguria that was established by M. Fulvius Flaccus (cos. 125 B.C.) along the Via Fulvia, a road running from Dertona to Hasta Pompeia.

properties.title: Forum Iulii Iriensium
properties.description: Iria/Forum Iulii Iriensium (modern Voghera) was located along the road from Placentia to Dertona. An Augustan colony was established there (colonia Forum Iulium Iriensium) that was then destroyed in the first century AD.

properties.title: Forum Livii
properties.description: A town of Gallia Cispadana located on the right bank of the Montone river.

properties.title: Forum Neronis
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 15 E1 Carpentorate/*Forum Neronis

properties.title: Forum Novum
properties.description: Forum Novum was a Roman town and an early medieval bishopric. It is located in the Sabine hills and was likely founded as a market center during the Republican period. It had achieved the status of municipium by the first century A.D.

properties.title: Forum Popilii
properties.description: Forum Popilii on the Via Aemilia was founded in 173 BC by M. Popilius Laenas.

properties.title: Forum Popillii
properties.description: An important town of the Ager Falernus, not to be confused with Forum Popili, the modern Polla.

properties.title: Forum Segusiavorum
properties.description: Forum Segusiavorum (Feurs) was a Roman market town at the junction of several major road systems.

properties.title: Forum Sempronii
properties.description: An ancient Roman colony, founded in the time of Gaius Sempronius Gracchus.

properties.title: Fregenae
properties.description: An ancient town of Etruria, Fregenae was located between Alsium and the mouth of the river Tiber.

properties.title: Fulfinium
properties.description: A Roman municipal center and port.

properties.title: Gabala
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 68 A3 Gabala

properties.title: Genava
properties.description: Genava (modern Geneva) was originally an oppidum of the Allobroges that passed into Roman control after the campaigns of Iulius Caesar.

properties.title: Genua
properties.description: Genua was an ancient settlement of Liguria that came to be destroyed by Carthage in 209 BC. Thereafter it received Roman municipal privileges and was rebuilt.

properties.title: Genusia
properties.description: Genusia, modern Ginosa, was a city of the Peucetii located to the west of Tarentum.

properties.title: Geraistos
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 58 H2 Geraistos

properties.title: Gerunda
properties.description: A settlement of Tarraconensis located at the confluence of the Ter and the Onyar rivers, Gerunda was the chief town of the Gerundenses. Modern Girona in Spain.

properties.title: Gesoriacum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 B2 Gesoriacum/Bononia

properties.title: Glanum
properties.description: Glanum was a Celtic oppidum that became a Roman city; it was eventually abandoned in the third century AD.

properties.title: Gracchuris
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 D3 Ilurcis/Grac(ch)urris

properties.title: Hadriane
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 87 B4 Kabeira/Neocaesarea/Diospolis/Sebaste/Hadriane

properties.title: Hadrianopolis
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 54 B2 Hadrianopolis

properties.title: Halae
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 E3 Halai

properties.title: Halaesa
properties.description: Halaesa is located on the north coast of Sicily between between Kalakta and Kephaloidion. The city was founded in 403 B.C. by Archonides of Herbita. The city sided with Rome during the First Punic War and was made "libera et immunis".

properties.title: Haluntium
properties.description: An ancient settlement located between Tyndaris and Calacte. Some authors ascribe the foundation of the site to the Acarnanians.

properties.title: Harpasa
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 G2 Harpasa

properties.title: Hasta
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 39 C4 Hasta

properties.title: Helorus
properties.description: An ancient settlement of Sicily that was likely a colonial foundation of Syracuse.

properties.title: Hephaestia
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 A2 Hephaistia

properties.title: Heracleopolis Parva
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 74 G2 Herakleopolis Mikra/Sethroe

properties.title: Herdoniae
properties.description: Herdonia was an ancient city of Apulia located along the via Minucia. The city was the site of two major engagements of the Second Punic War (212 and 210 B.C., respectively), both of which were Carthaginian victories.

properties.title: Hermopolis Magna
properties.description: The capital city of the Hermopolite nome (the fifteenth Nome of Upper Egypt). The ancient Egyptians called the site Khmun after the group of eight divinities (Ogdoad) that represented the primordial world.

properties.title: Heroonpolis
properties.description: Heroonpolis, located at Tell el-Maskhuta, was an ancient Egyptian city of the Nile Delta.

properties.title: Hierakonpolis
properties.description: The ritual and political capital of Upper Egypt at the end of the Predynastic period and the Early Dynastic Period, with occupation beginning as early as Naqada I. The site was an important ritual center for the worship of Horus.

properties.title: Hierasykaminos
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 81 C2 Hierasykaminos

properties.title: Horreum Margi
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 21 D6 Horreum Margi

properties.title: Hyampolis
properties.description: Hyampolis was a city of eastern Phocis, located some 2.5 km from Abai.

properties.title: Hydissa
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 F3 Hydissos

properties.title: Hydruntum
properties.description: A Greek city at the easternmost point of the Italian peninsula that sided against Rome in both the Pyrrhic and Hannibalic wars.

properties.title: Hyllarima
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 G2 Hyllarima

properties.title: Hypselis
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 E2 Hypsele/Schotep

properties.title: Iatinum
properties.description: Iatinon/Fixtuinum was a city of the the city of the Meldi, a people of Gallia Lugdunensis.

properties.title: Idebessus
properties.description: Idebessos was an ancient city of Lycia.

properties.title: Iesso
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 G4 Ies(s)os

properties.title: Iliberri
properties.description: An ancient settlement located to the west of modern Granada. Its precise location is unknown.

properties.title: Ilipa Magna
properties.description: Ilipa Magna was located on the right bank of the Baetis river. It is now the modern Alcalá del Río. In antiquity it was notable for silver mines located nearby.

properties.title: Imbros
properties.description: An ancient settlement (modern Kaleköy on the Turkish island of Gökçeada).

properties.title: Industria
properties.description: Industria/Bodincomagum? (Monteu da Po) was a Ligurian settlement in the Po valley. Under Roman influence the site became known as Industria, a colonial establishment, in regio IX of the Augustan organization of Italy.

properties.title: Interpromium
properties.description: A village of the Marrucini, forming a station on the Via Claudia Valeria between Corfinium and Teate.

properties.title: Ios
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 A4 Ios

properties.title: Ioulis
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 58 G2 Ioulis

properties.title: Isaura Vetus
properties.description: An ancient settlement, modern Bozkır in Turkey.

properties.title: Iulia Equestris
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 18 D3 Col. Iulia Equestris/Noviodunum

properties.title: Iuliobona
properties.description: A settlement of the Caletes in Gallia Belgica

properties.title: Iulium Carnicum
properties.description: Iulium Carnicum (modern Zuglio) was a Roman settlement built atop an indigenous settlement after 52 BC. It was a dependency of Aquileia.

properties.title: Katabolos
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 67 B3 Katabolos/Moutloubake

properties.title: Kimolos
properties.description: An ancient settlement on the Cycladic island of Kimolos, located at modern Ag. Andreas (now a small island in the western bay of Kimolos).

properties.title: Kotyora
properties.description: An ancient settlement on the Black Sea coast just to the north of modern Ordu, Turkey. Archaeological remnants of port facilities were previously noted in the neighborhood of Kirazlimanı.

properties.title: Kythnos
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 58 G3 Kythnos

properties.title: Labicum
properties.description: Originally a member of the Latin League, the city of Labicum was taken by the Romans ca. 418 B.C. By the time of Augustus, the site was almost certainly deserted and its precise location remains debated today.

properties.title: Labitolosa
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 F3 Labitulosa

properties.title: Lacimurga
properties.description: Following TIR, the Barrington Atlas tentatively placed this ancient settlement at the archaeological site in Spain known today as Cerro del Cogolludo, which is located near the modern town of Navalvillar de Pela.

properties.title: Lacobriga
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 24 G2 Lacobriga

properties.title: Laertes
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 66 A3 Laertes

properties.title: Larisa
properties.description: An ancient city was located near modern Buruncuk, 28 km north of Smyrna (modern Izmir) in Turkey. Some scholars identify it as the "Pelasgian" Laris(s)a in Aeolis mentioned by Homer, Xenophon, Herodotus, and Strabo, but that identification is disputed.

properties.title: Laus Pompeia
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 39 E3 Laus Pompeia

properties.title: Lebedus
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 D1 Lebedos/Ptolemais

properties.title: Leontopolis
properties.description: An ancient settlement in Egypt, known as Leontopolis (one of two in the area) in Greco-Roman sources.

properties.title: Libarna
properties.description: Libarna was a Roman city located in Liguria.

properties.title: Lindinis
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 8 E4 Lindinis

properties.title: Lindus
properties.description: Lindos is an ancient city located on the island of Rhodes.

properties.title: Liternum
properties.description: An ancient city of Campania located near the mouth of the Volturnus river; a Roman colony was established at Liternum in 194 B.C. The site is most famous as the location of the villa of Scipio Africanus, as described by Seneca.

properties.title: Lopodunum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 12 B3 Lopodunum

properties.title: Lopsica
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 20 B5 Lopsica

properties.title: Lousoi
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 58 C2 Lousoi

properties.title: Lousonna
properties.description: Lousonna began as a Roman military establishment on the site of a Celtic center.

properties.title: Lucus Augusti
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 E4 Lucus Augusti

properties.title: Lugdunum
properties.description: Lugdunum Batavorum, also referred to as Brittenburg, is the site of Roman ruins near Katwijk aan Zee that emerged in the sixteenth century at the mouth of the Oude Rijn (old river Rhine).

properties.title: Lutetia
properties.description: Lutetia (modern Paris) was the capital of the Parisii, a tribe of ancient Gaul.

properties.title: Lydai
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 65 A4 Lydai

properties.title: Macynia
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 B4 Makyneia

properties.title: Mantua
properties.description: Mantua (modern Mantova) is a city of Etruscan origin that received Roman citizenship in 49 B.C.

properties.title: Maracitana
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 33 D1 *Maracitana

properties.title: Marathus
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 68 A4 Marathos

properties.title: Mariana
properties.description: Mariana was a Roman city on the east coast of Corsica founded by Caius Marius ca. 93 BC.

properties.title: Mariana
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 27 C3 Mariana

properties.title: Maronea
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 51 F3 Maroneia

properties.title: Mauves
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 D1 Mauves

properties.title: Maxula
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 32 F3 Maxula

properties.title: Mediolanum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 18 B2 [Mediolanum]

properties.title: Mediolanum Aulercorum
properties.description: Mediolanum Aulercorum was the chief town of the Aulerci Eburovices.

properties.title: Melos
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 58 G4 Melos

properties.title: Metelis
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 74 C2 Metelis

properties.title: Metellinum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 26 E3 Metellinum

properties.title: Methymna
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 C3 Methymna

properties.title: Missua
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 32 G3 Missua

properties.title: Mylae
properties.description: An ancient Greek settlement on the north-east coast of Sicily, located at the base of a peninsula that juts into the Tyrrhenian Sea. A colony of Zancle, it was probably founded in the eighth or seventh century BC.

properties.title: Myndus
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 E3 Myndos

properties.title: Myos Hormos
properties.description: Myos Hormos was a port on the Red Sea built by the Ptolemies in the third century BC.

properties.title: Myrina
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 D4 Myrina/Sebastopolis

properties.title: Namnetum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 D1 Civitas Namnetum/Kondeouinkon?

properties.title: Naryca
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 D3 Naryka/Tarphe?

properties.title: Nasium
properties.modern: Naix aux Forges and Saint Amand sur Ornain
properties.description: A large ancient settlement located in the vicinity of modern Naix-aux-Forges and Saint-Armand-sur-Ornain in France. Excavations began in the 19th century and continued in the 20th.

properties.title: Naxus
properties.description: Settlement on the Aegean island of Naxos.

properties.title: Nedinum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 20 C5 Nedinum

properties.title: Nemetacum
properties.description: Nemetacum was the principal center of the Atrebates.

properties.title: Neretum
properties.description: An ancient settlement of the Messapii in Apulia captured by the Romans in 269 BC. The Trajanic extension of the Via Appia ran through the site.

properties.title: Nesactium
properties.description: Nesactium was an ancient town of Histria.

properties.title: Netum
properties.description: Neaition/Netum was an ancient town in Sicily that likely became subject to Syracuse after 263 B.C.

properties.title: Nicopolis
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 87 D4 Nicopolis

properties.title: Nisa
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 65 C5 Nisa

properties.title: Nisyros
properties.description: The eponymous settlement of the volcanic island of Nisyros in the Aegean Sea.

properties.title: Nomentum
properties.description: The Latin town of Nomentum and Rome were linked by means of the Via Nomentana. In Livy's history the town, a member of the Latin League, is captured by Lucius Tarquinius Priscus.

properties.title: Norbensis Caesarina
properties.description: An Roman settlement, modern Cáceres in Spain.

properties.title: Noviodunum Diablintum
properties.description: Noiodounon Diablintum is a settlement with significant extant remains, located at modern Jublains in France.

properties.title: Noviomagus
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 I3 Noviomagus/Nemetae

properties.title: Noviomagus Lexoviorum
properties.description: Noviomagus Lexoviorum, located at the confluence of the Orbiquet and Touques rivers, was the chief settlement of the Lexovii.

properties.title: Numana
properties.description: Numana was a settlement of ancient Picenum located south of Ancona.

properties.title: Numistro
properties.description: A town in Lucania that was the location of a military engagement between Hannibal and Marcus Claudius Marcellus in 210 B.C.

properties.title: Olba
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 66 D3 Olba

properties.title: Olcinium
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 49 B2 Olcinium/Vicinium

properties.title: Onchesmus
properties.description: Onchesmos was a port in Epirus.

properties.title: Opitergium
properties.description: Opitergium (modern Oderzo) is a settlement of the Veneti along the Piave river located at a junction of the Via Postumia.

properties.title: Oricus
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 49 B3 Orikon

properties.title: Orippo
properties.description: A Roman city with Turdetanian origins. It is cited on both the Antonine Itinerary and the Vicarello goblets.

properties.title: Ossigi
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 27 B4 Ossigi

properties.title: Palaiapolis
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 G3 Kys/Kanebion/Palaiapolis

properties.title: Pallantium
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 58 C3 Pallantion

properties.title: Panopolis
properties.description: Capital city of the ninth (Chemmite) nome of Upper Egypt.

properties.title: Paros
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 A3 Paros

properties.title: Passaron
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 54 C2 Passaron

properties.title: Pedona
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 39 A5 Pedo(na)

properties.title: Pelinna
properties.description: A major city of western Thessaly.

properties.title: Perdices
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 34 D2 Perdices?

properties.title: Petras Megas
properties.description: Petras Megas (known as Bardia or Bórdi Slemán) is a port settlement located in eastern Libya.

properties.title: Petras Mikros
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: None

properties.title: Petuaria
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 8 G1 *Petuaria

properties.title: Phalanna
properties.description: Orthe/Phalanna was a city in Perrhaebia in Thessaly.

properties.title: Phasaelis
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 70 G1 Phasaelis

properties.title: Phellus
properties.description: A Lycian settlement located near modern Çukurbağ.

properties.title: Pollentia
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 39 B4 Pollentia

properties.title: Pompelo
properties.description: Cn. Pompeius Magnus is considered to have been the founder of Pompelo (modern Pamplona) while on campaign against Sertorius in 75 BC.

properties.title: Pons Aeni
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 E2 Pons Aeni

properties.title: Pons Saravi
properties.description: A road station between Tarquimpol and Saverne.

properties.title: Portus Blendium
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 24 G1 Portus Blendium

properties.title: Portus Magnus
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 29 E1 Portus Magnus

properties.title: Potidaion
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 60 E4 Potidaion

properties.title: Praesidium Sufative
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 29 D1 Albulae/Praesidium Sufative

properties.title: Praetorium
properties.description: An ancient settlement, at Thamad Hassan in Libya, cited: BAtlas 37 A1 Praetorium

properties.title: Raetinium
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 20 C5 Raetinium?

properties.title: Ratiatum
properties.description: A site in the Loire estuary that served as a port.

properties.title: Regium Lepidum
properties.description: Regium Lepidum (modern Reggio Emilia) was founded by M. Aemilius Lepidus in either 187 or 175 B.C.

properties.title: Rhodiapolis
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 65 D5 Rhodia(polis)

properties.title: Roda
properties.description: A Greek trading settlement in northeastern Spain founded by the Rhodians.

properties.title: Rotomagus
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 B3 Rotomagus

properties.title: Rubi
properties.description: Rubi was a statio in Apulia, located along the Via Minucia.

properties.title: Rudiae
properties.description: An ancient Messapic town, Rudiae was the birthplace of the poet Ennius.

properties.title: S(...)
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 21 B6 S(...)

properties.title: Salapia Nova
properties.description: Sal(a)pia(i)/Salinae was an ancient city of Daunia.

properties.title: Salapia Vetus
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 45 C2 Sal(a)pia(i)/Elpia

properties.title: Saletio
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 I4 Saletio

properties.title: Samarobriva Ambianorum
properties.description: A city of Gallia Belgica located on the Somme that is mentioned in Caesar's commentaries.

properties.title: Satala
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 G4 Satala

properties.title: Scolacium Minervia
properties.description: Scylletium/Scolacium was an ancient settlement in Bruttium located on the Gulf of Squillace.

properties.title: Segobriga
properties.description: Segobriga was an Ibero-Roman city.

properties.title: Seleucia Pieria
properties.description: The capital city of Seleucus I Nicator, established ca. 300 BC.

properties.title: Selinous
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 F2 Selinous

properties.title: Sidrona
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 20 C5 Sidrona

properties.title: Silvium
properties.description: Silvium (modern Gravina in Puglia) was founded as a Greek colony. It was a town on the frontier of the Peucetii.

properties.title: Simena
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 65 C5 Simena

properties.title: Singilia Barba
properties.description: Singilia Barba was the site of a Roman municipium near Antequera, Málaga, Spain.

properties.title: Sisapo
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 27 A3 Sisapo?

properties.title: Soura
properties.description: An ancient settlement, now known as al-Suriyya in Syria.

properties.title: Suana
properties.description: Suana (Sovana) has Etruscan origins but later became a Roman municipium. In the fifth century A.D. it became an episcopal see.

properties.title: Sumelocenna
properties.description: Sumelocenna was a Roman settlement on the Neckar river that was the seat of the procurator of the saltus Sumelocennensis during the late first century AD.

properties.title: Surrentum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 44 F4 Surrentum

properties.title: Syros
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 60 A5 Syros

properties.title: Tarquinii
properties.description: An ancient city of Etruria and the leading city of the doudecim populi Etruriae, Tarquinia (called Tarch(u)na or Tarchna in Etruscan) was a major urban center of ancient Italy.

properties.title: Tarvenna
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 C2 Tarvenna

properties.title: Tarvisium
properties.description: Tarvisium (Treviso) is a settlement located in the plain of the Silis river, not far from the Via Claudia Augusta.

properties.title: Teanum Sidicinum
properties.description: Teanum Sidicinum (modern Teano) was the capital of the Oscan-speaking Sidicini tribe. The city likely submitted to Rome after 334 B.C.

properties.title: Tebtunis
properties.description: An ancient Egyptian settlement located near the modern village of Tell Umm el-Baragat in the Al Fayyum Governorate. Tebtunis was founded by Amenemhat III around 1800 BC and flourished during the Ptolemaic period.

properties.title: Tegianum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 45 C4 Tegianum

properties.title: Tempsa
properties.description: An ancient city located on the west coast of Bruttium, just north of the Gulf of Hipponium.

properties.title: Tenedos
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 65 E4 Tenedos

properties.title: Tenos
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 60 B4 Tenos

properties.title: Terenouthis
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 74 D4 Terenouthis

properties.title: Termes
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 B4 Termes

properties.title: Teuthrone
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 58 C4 Teuthrone

properties.title: Thamusida
properties.description: A Roman city located in the province of Mauretania Tingitana, along the road from Tingi to Sala. The city is mentioned by Ptolemy, the Antonine Itinerary, and the Geographer of Ravenna.

properties.title: Theangela
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 F3 Theangela

properties.title: Themiskyra
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 87 B3 Themiskyra

properties.title: Thera
properties.description: An ancient settlement located atop the modern Messavouno mountain on the island that shared its name in antiquity (modern Thira/Santorini).

properties.title: Thinis
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 D1 Thynis/Thone

properties.title: Thuburbo Minus
properties.description: A settlement in Africa Proconsularis, mentioned in the Itinerarium Antonini and the Tabula Peutingeriana.

properties.title: Thunusidum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 32 C4 Thunusuda

properties.title: Ticinum
properties.description: An ancient city of Gallia Transpadana, Ticinum lay along the line of the ancient Via Aemilia. Today Ticinum is the modern city of Pavia.

properties.title: Tiddis
properties.description: *Tiddis (or Castellum Tidditanorum) was a Roman city located in the Constantine Province in Algeria.

properties.title: Tragurium
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 20 D6 Tragurium

properties.title: Traianopolis
properties.description: Selinous/Traianopolis was a city of Cilicia Aspera and counted among the chief coastal cities of Cilicia. It is perhaps best known as the place where Trajan died in A.D. 117 en route to Rome from his campaigns in the East.

properties.title: Trebula Suffenas
properties.description: An ancient Sabine city.

properties.title: Tullum
properties.description: Tullum was the capital of the civitas Leucorum.

properties.title: Tunisa
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 32 F2 Thinisa?/Tuneiza?/Tunisa?

properties.title: Turiaso
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 D4 Turiaso

properties.title: Uxama
properties.description: Uxama Argaela was a Celtiberian settlement of the Arevaci. The site was subsequently conquered by the Romans.

properties.title: Uxentum
properties.description: Uzentum/Ouxenton (Ugento) was an ancient city in the territory of the Salentini with both Messapic and Roman phases.

properties.title: Uzalis
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 32 F2 Uzalis

properties.title: Uzelis
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 31 E4 *Uzelis

properties.title: Vada Sabatia
properties.description: An ancient settlement that began as a second century B.C. Roman military outpost.

properties.title: Vappincum
properties.description: Vappincum was a pre-Roman settlement captured by the Romans in 14 BC.

properties.title: Vardagate
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 39 C3 Vardagate

properties.title: Varvaria
properties.description: A Roman municipium of the first century A.D.

properties.title: Vasio
properties.description: Vasio (modern Vaison-la-Romaine) was the civitas capital of the Vocontii and eventually received Roman status by the late first century BC.

properties.title: Vegium
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 20 C5 Vegium

properties.title: Veleia
properties.description: Veleia was an ancient Ligurian settlement (regio VIII).

properties.title: Verecunda
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 34 E2 Verecunda

properties.title: Veretum
properties.description: Veretum/Baris was a town of Calabria, in the territory of the Sallentines.

properties.title: Vertillum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 18 B2 *Vertillum?

properties.title: Vibinum
properties.description: Vibinum/Vibarna was an ancient settlement in Campania that opposed Rome during the Samnite Wars.

properties.title: Vindobriga
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 G2 Vendoeuvres-en-Brenne

properties.title: Virovesca
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 B3 Virovesca

properties.title: Volcei
properties.description: Volcei (modern Buccino) was an ancient city of Lucania that was subjugated by the Romans in 209 BC.

properties.title: Vorgium
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 7 C3 Vorgium

properties.title: Xois
properties.description: Ancient Xois was located on an island that was formed by the Sebennytic and Phatnitic branches of the Nile. It became the capital of its own nome, the Xoite nome.

properties.title: Zacynthus
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 54 inset Zacynthus

properties.title: Zela
properties.description: Zela (modern Zile, Turkey) was an important ancient city of north-central Asia Minor; Strabo claimed Semiramis had founded the site. Iulius Caesar prevailed in the Battle of Zela against Pharnaces II of The Kingdom of Pontus.
